title: PokéAPI Resources, URL API, Pagination Shapes, and Fair-Use Policy · Contract Lens: Pokedex Ts Resource Input
summary: User input becomes a full fetch destination, URL strings are concatenated, pagination fields are assumed present, and repeated commands bypass the API request to cache every resource. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts resource input increment in fixed-origin typed PokéAPI request policy. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Validate resource kind, numeric identifier or canonical constrained name, and preserve requested versus resolved identity.
Hold scheme and authority in trusted configuration and map commands only to enumerated PokéAPI resource paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Resource Input
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 4845 during a clean emitted-package smoke project. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit fixed-origin PokéAPI URLs, validated pagination shapes, and fair-use request budgets; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate resource kind, numeric identifier or canonical constrained name, and preserve requested versus resolved identity.
